grade) · BIS IS 2660 compliant (technical grade) · MSDS available · GST 12% applicable
Section 2 — The India-specific context: three types of urea, three very different rules
Most buyers outside India are surprised to learn that in India, urea is not one product — it is three legally distinct products with different import rules, tax rates and end-use regulations.

Agricultural urea is a subsidised fertiliser sold to farmers at a controlled price of ₹242 for a 45kg bag. Import of agricultural urea is canalised exclusively through government State Trading Enterprises — MMTC, STC and RCF — based on global tendering. Private traders cannot import or sell agricultural urea. This is a legal restriction, not a commercial preference.
Technical grade urea is the term used in India for industrial urea — the same chemical compound (

NH₂)₂) but without neem coating, without subsidy, and with stricter purity controls. Private traders can import and sell technical grade urea to industrial buyers. GST is 12% vs 5% for fertiliser grade. This is what we supply.
grade urea (also called automotive grade or DEF grade) is a sub-category of technical grade urea with even tighter purity specifications — used specifically for making Diesel Exhaust Fluid (DEF /
can also be imported and traded by private companies

industrial grade or chemical grade urea. In India, the term "technical grade" has become standard in trade and government documentation to distinguish it from subsidised fertiliser urea. When an Indian chemical plant, resin manufacturer,
adhesive company says "we need technical grade urea" — they mean non-fertiliser, non-subsidised, high-purity industrial urea without neem coating.
is mandatorily coated with neem oil (minimum 0.035%) under government regulations — introduced to slow nitrogen release and prevent diversion of subsidised urea to industrial use
. This neem coating makes fertiliser urea completely unsuitable for industrial processes — it contaminates resins, damages
SCR systems and interferes with chemical reactions.
Technical grade urea has no neem coating — it is clean, white, odourless granules or

with controlled chemistry suitable for industrial use. This one difference — neem vs no neem — is the single most practical distinguishing feature between fertiliser and technical grade urea in India. Any Indian industrial buyer who has accidentally received neem-coated fertiliser urea instead of technical grade urea understands immediately why the distinction matters.

grade: DEF is a precise mixture of 32.5% urea and 67.5% deionised water — using low-quality or agricultural grade urea can introduce impurities that damage DEF injectors, sensors and catalysts, leading to poor engine performance and compliance failures. The heavy metals limits in ISO 22241 are extremely strict — even trace levels of
iron or chromium can destroy an SCR catalyst.

Urea-formaldehyde (UF) resin manufacturers — largest industrial buyer segment; UF resin used in plywood, MDF, particleboard, furniture panels; India has 500+ plywood and panel board manufacturers; urea is a primary raw material
Melamine-urea-formaldehyde (MUF) resin — premium panel boards, laminates, surface coatings; consistent biuret level essential for reliable resin performance
Adhesive and binder manufacturers — wood adhesives, construction adhesives, textile binders
Animal feed manufacturers — non-protein nitrogen source in cattle and buffalo feed; large dairy industry in Gujarat, UP, Punjab
Pharmaceutical and cosmetics — urea as penetration enhancer in topical creams, moisturisers, dermatological preparations
Dyes and pigment industry — intermediate in certain dye synthesis pathways
NOx reduction (SNCR systems) — power plants, cement kilns, waste incinerators use aqueous urea solution for Selective Non-Catalytic Reduction of nitrogen oxides — growing compliance-driven market
/ DEF blending plants — mix urea with deionised water at 32.5% to produce
; dozens of blending units across India in industrial clusters
for Tata Motors, Ashok Leyland, Mahindra, Volvo India, Mercedes trucks; all BS-VI compliant vehicles require
Fleet operators and fuel distributors — large truck fleets, logistics companies, mining operators; bulk institutional buyers
Industrial SCR systems — large diesel generators, marine engines, industrial boilers with SCR emission control
BS-VI compliance tail-wind: India's BS-VI transition (2020 nationwide) created permanent structural demand for
grade urea that did not exist at scale before
grade urea — but not agricultural urea
is frequently asked and frequently misunderstood

The reason for this distinction is straightforward: agricultural urea is subsidised by the government — allowing private traders to import it would create diversion risk. Technical grade urea carries no subsidy, so private import and trade
China — world's largest urea producer; Shandong, Shanxi and Inner Mongolia plants; competitive pricing, consistent quality; primary source for industrial grade
urea; good logistics to India; one of India's preferred sources
Russia — large-scale producer; competitive FOB pricing;
; proximity advantage; active exporters of technical grade
Fixed origin source — consistent purity lot to lot
Full COA per lot: urea %, N %, biuret %, moisture, metals (

MSDS, COO, packing list with every consignment
MOQ 2 MT — easy trial before full container
Competitive CIF / DAP pricing to your plant
India is one of Asia's most open and fastest-growing markets for industrial urea — technical and
can both be imported and traded freely by private companies
. BS-VI norms have created permanent structural demand for

urea and the resin and panel board industry is one of India's fastest-growing manufacturing segments. Both buy regularly and consistently — a real opportunity for suppliers who want a stable, long-term India channel.
We prefer to build supplier relationships on credit-based
— suppliers who work this way are welcome to reach out.
Technical grade: Urea ≥99.0% · N ≥46% · Biuret ≤1.0% · Moisture ≤0.5% · No neem coating · COA per lot
grade: ISO 22241 compliant · Biuret ≤0.3% · Heavy metals within ISO limits ·

Ports: JNPT / Nhava Sheva, Mundra, Hazira, Chennai
Transit: China 15–20 days · Middle East / Oman 8–12 days · Russia 20–25 days
HS Code: 3102.10 (urea, whether or not in aqueous solution)
Inland delivery to: resin clusters (Ahmedabad, Pune, Chennai) ·
Lead time: ex-stock 3–7 days · fresh import 25–40 days depending on origin
Q1. What is technical grade urea?
Industrial urea without neem coating, used for chemicals and manufacturing.
Not the same as subsidised fertiliser urea in India.
Q2. Can private companies import urea in India?
can be freely imported and sold
It contains higher biuret and impurities.
RTGS against loading or can be discussed depending
. What type of suppliers do we work with?
regular shipments and long-term supply programs
Q8. What quantity can we handle?
for Indian industrial buyers, not one-time shipments.
Q9. Can new suppliers collaborate with us?
new suppliers looking to enter India
with consistent quality and competitive pricing.
